sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ip inet NOT NULL, value DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL);
SELECT id, value, log2(value) AS log_value FROM test_table WHERE log2(value) <= log2(32.0);
SELECT id, value, log2(value) AS log_value FROM test_table WHERE value <= (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_table);
SELECT id, value FROM (SELECT id, value, log2(value) AS log_value FROM test_table) AS subquery WHERE log_value IS NOT NULL AND value <= 64.0;
CREATE TABLE second_table (fk_id INT REFERENCES test_table(id), description VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);

INSERT INTO second_table (fk_id, description) VALUES (1, 'Sample entry 1'), (2, 'Sample entry exceeding length limit because of an extremely verbose description that would normally get truncated.'), (3, 'Sample entry with NULL'), (4, 'Sample 4');
SELECT a.id, a.value, log2(a.value) AS log_value, b.description FROM test_table a JOIN second_table b ON a.id = b.fk_id WHERE a.value <= log2(32.0) AND b.description IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL OR log2(value) IS NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT id, value FROM test_table WHERE value <> 0) AS distinct_values;
SELECT AVG(log2(value)) OVER (PARTITION BY id) AS avg_log_value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL HAVING avg_log_value <= log2(32.0);
